key_insights.insight: Speaking and listening are skills, not innate capabilities, and mastering them significantly impacts happiness, effectiveness, and well-being.
key_insights.quote: Speaking and listening are skills that we do not teach in school or university, which is mad. So we have to take it upon ourselves because they matter, you know, they affect our outcomes in life.

key_insights.insight: Treating your voice as a skill is the first step to improving your communication.
key_insights.quote: Treating your voice as a skill is the first thing. So becoming conscious that this is a skill.

key_insights.insight: Deep breathing is crucial for effective speaking and can calm nerves.
key_insights.quote: With your voice, it's very important to breathe deeply and to get into that practice. Also a great cure for nerves.

key_insights.insight: Variety in speech, particularly intonation, is essential to avoid sounding monotonous and to convey emotion.
key_insights.quote: So it's understanding how to, it's not just reading a script flat, it's putting your personality into what you're saying. And that makes all the difference in the world.

key_insights.insight: Prosody, which includes intonation and rhythm, is key to adding personality to your speech.
key_insights.quote: Prosody is both intonation. So the up and down delivery, which is route one for emotion. It's, it's absolutely crucial in speaking. And it's also the rhythm of your speaking, the gaps you leave and the emphasis you put on words.

key_insights.insight: Being comfortable with silence and avoiding verbal tics enhances the quality of speech.
key_insights.quote: It's about being comfortable with silence, for example, not filling every tiny little gap with ums, errs, you knows, you know what I means, verbal tics.

key_insights.insight: Recording and reviewing your own speech allows for continuous improvement and mastery of vocal skills.
key_insights.quote: It's quite important to record yourself, listen back and start to take it as a skill and as mastery, become your own coach effectively.

key_insights.insight: It's important to adjust your speech to cultural norms, considering the level of prosody appropriate for the audience.
key_insights.quote: You have to be adjusting to the prosody or prosody of the audience you're speaking to.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice resonant breathing to improve breath control and calm nerves.
actionable_advice.how_to: Breathe in through your nose and out through your mouth as if blowing, lengthening the in-breath and out-breath gradually.

actionable_advice.advice: Record yourself speaking and listen back to identify areas for improvement.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use a recording device to capture your speech, then analyze it for verbal tics, monotonous delivery, or other areas needing refinement.

actionable_advice.advice: Consciously incorporate variety in your speech to maintain listener engagement.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pay attention to intonation, rhythm, and emphasis on words to avoid a flat or monotonous delivery.
Explore yoga or other techniques and focus on breathing from your diaphragm.

memorable_stories: The speaker shares an anecdote about giving a talk in Finland and misinterpreting the audience's quiet applause as a sign of failure.
contrarian_claims: Formal vocal training is often overlooked by people in positions where public speaking is a key part of their job.
Speaking and listening are not taught adequately in schools, despite their importance.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 85 - How To Speak So EVERYONE Listens To You: Julian Treasure
